assertions.title: Login Finish Verifies Credential and Issues Session (Re-login)
assertions.description: POST /auth/login/finish with a valid WebAuthn assertion response verifies the credential against stored public key and issues new session cookies. This is the CRITICAL bug fix - re-login must work.

steps.expected: HTTP 200 OK with publicKey containing allowCredentials array
steps.observed: HTTP 200 OK with publicKey.allowCredentials containing 1 credential
Successfully created assertion with credential ID
HTTP 200 OK, response: {ok: true, user: {id: '...', email: '...'}}

steps.expected: choir_access and choir_refresh cookies set (HttpOnly, SameSite)
steps.observed: Both cookies present with correct attributes - HttpOnly: true, SameSite: Lax

steps.expected: Returns authenticated: true with user data

evidence.databaseVerification: credential sign_count updated from 0 to 1 after login

assertions.title: Full Flow - Register → Login → Logout → Re-login
assertions.description: Complete user journey: Register with passkey, logout completely, then successfully re-login with the SAME passkey without re-registration.

steps.expected: Auth entry page displayed with Register/Sign In options
steps.observed: Auth page loaded with email input and passkey buttons

steps.action: Register with unique email using virtual authenticator
Verify login succeeds and session is established
steps.observed: Session confirmed authenticated with correct user email
Session shows authenticated: false as expected

steps.action: Re-login with same email and same passkey (CRITICAL TEST)

steps.expected: GET /auth/session returns authenticated: true with same user
steps.observed: Session authenticated with same user ID and email as original registration

frictions.description: Auth database file (auth.db) was initially 0 bytes (empty), causing all credential storage to fail with 'failed to store credential' error. Required restarting the auth service to properly initialize the database schema.
frictions.resolution: Killed existing auth processes, removed empty auth.db, restarted auth service which created proper schema. Tests then passed.

summary: Both assertions PASSED. The critical re-login bug fix is verified working: users can register with a passkey, logout, and successfully re-login with the same passkey without re-registration. The login finish endpoint correctly verifies the credential against the stored public key and issues new session cookies.
